title: Translate the patient explainer (after high-risk sign-off)
context: After the patient explainer (patient-explainer-020) has been fully signed off by both an oncologist and a patient advocate, translations can be commissioned to reach non-English-speaking patients and advocates. This task is strictly gated: it must not begin until patient-explainer-020 is shipped (both sign-offs on file, 'not medical advice' framing confirmed). Each translation must preserve the 'not medical advice' notice and must not introduce new medical or legal claims. High risk (riskTier:high) because patient-facing content with 'not medical advice' notice must be preserved accurately across languages. Each translation should ideally have a qualified bilingual reviewer (the target-language equivalent of the oncologist/advocate sign-off, or at minimum a bilingual review by someone familiar with the cancer context).
objective: Translate the signed-off patient explainer (patient-explainer-020) into one or more target languages, preserving the 'not medical advice' notice and semantic fidelity, with a named bilingual reviewer sign-off for each language and a committed sign-off artifact — strictly gated on patient-explainer-020 being shipped first.
acceptanceCriteria: This task does not begin until patient-explainer-020 is shipped with both oncologist and advocate sign-offs recorded; the dependency is a hard prerequisite.
Each translation preserves the 'not medical advice' notice in the target language, verbatim or equivalent, in the same prominent position as the source.
No new medical claims, prognosis, or treatment guidance are introduced by the translation; the translated content is reviewed against the source for semantic fidelity.

Each language translation has a bilingual reviewer (ideally a qualified person familiar with the cancer/health context in the target language) and the reviewer is named in a committed sign-off artifact.
The translated documents are committed alongside the source; outputLicense CC-BY-4.0; DCO signed-off.
output: Committed translations of the patient explainer into one or more target languages, each with the 'not medical advice' notice preserved and a bilingual reviewer sign-off, licensed CC-BY-4.0.
